comment_text.sn44.9:2.1: Compare the similar passage at MN 77:6.3, which also introduces the six ascetic teachers.
comment_text.sn44.9:5.4: The simile of the fire going out for lack of fuel is famously discussed with Vacchagotta in MN 72.
comment_text.sn44.9:6.4: This is one of several passages that acknowledge an “in-between state” after death and before rebirth. See also SN 12.11:1.3, SN 35.87:11.8, SN 46.3:9.5. | In parallel phrases, aññataraṁ qualifies the realm of rebirth (eg. AN 6.34:1.5: aññataraṁ brahmalokaṁ upapanno). This must be the meaning of the second kāya here, rather than “body”. | I read satto as “attached”, since one who has been freed would not be reborn.

root_text.sn44.9:1.1: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn44.9:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn44.9:2.1: “Purimāni, bho gotama, divasāni purimatarāni sambahulānaṁ nānātitthiyānaṁ samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ kutūhalasālāyaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.sn44.9:2.2: ‘ayaṁ kho pūraṇo kassapo saṅghī ceva gaṇī ca gaṇācariyo ca ñāto yasassī titthakaro sādhusammato bahujanassa.
root_text.sn44.9:2.3: Sopi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
root_text.sn44.9:2.5: Yopissa sāvako uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto tampi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
root_text.sn44.9:3.5: ayampi kho ajito kesakambalo saṅghī ceva gaṇī ca gaṇācariyo ca ñāto yasassī titthakaro sādhusammato bahujanassa.
root_text.sn44.9:3.6: Sopi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
root_text.sn44.9:3.8: Yopissa sāvako uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto tampi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
root_text.sn44.9:4.1: Ayampi kho samaṇo gotamo saṅghī ceva gaṇī ca gaṇācariyo ca ñāto yasassī titthakaro sādhusammato bahujanassa.
root_text.sn44.9:4.2: Sopi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
root_text.sn44.9:4.4: Yopissa sāvako uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto tañca sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu na byākaroti:
root_text.sn44.9:4.6: Api ca kho naṁ evaṁ byākaroti:
root_text.sn44.9:4.7: “acchecchi taṇhaṁ, vivattayi saṁyojanaṁ, sammā mānābhisamayā antamakāsi dukkhassā”’ti.
root_text.sn44.9:4.8: Tassa mayhaṁ, bho gotama, ahu deva kaṅkhā, ahu vicikicchā:
root_text.sn44.9:5.2: Kaṅkhanīye ca pana te ṭhāne vicikicchā uppannā.
root_text.sn44.9:5.3: Saupādānassa khvāhaṁ, vaccha, upapattiṁ paññāpemi no anupādānassa.
root_text.sn44.9:5.4: Seyyathāpi, vaccha, aggi saupādāno jalati, no anupādāno;
root_text.sn44.9:5.5: evameva khvāhaṁ, vaccha, saupādānassa upapattiṁ paññāpemi, no anupādānassā”ti.
root_text.sn44.9:6.1: “Yasmiṁ, bho gotama, samaye acci vātena khittā dūrampi gacchati, imassa pana bhavaṁ gotamo kiṁ upādānasmiṁ paññāpetī”ti?
root_text.sn44.9:6.2: “Yasmiṁ kho, vaccha, samaye acci vātena khittā dūrampi gacchati, tamahaṁ vātūpādānaṁ paññāpemi.
root_text.sn44.9:6.3: Vāto hissa, vaccha, tasmiṁ samaye upādānaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.sn44.9:6.4: “Yasmiñca pana, bho gotama, samaye imañca kāyaṁ nikkhipati, satto ca aññataraṁ kāyaṁ anupapanno hoti, imassa pana bhavaṁ gotamo kiṁ upādānasmiṁ paññāpetī”ti?
root_text.sn44.9:6.5: “Yasmiṁ kho, vaccha, samaye imañca kāyaṁ nikkhipati, satto ca aññataraṁ kāyaṁ anupapanno hoti, tamahaṁ taṇhūpādānaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.sn44.9:6.6: Taṇhā hissa, vaccha, tasmiṁ samaye upādānaṁ hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn44.9:1.1: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta went up to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn44.9:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn44.9:2.1: “Worthy Gotama, a few days ago several ascetics, brahmins, and wanderers who follow various other religions were sitting together in the debating hall, and this discussion came up among them:
translation_text.sn44.9:2.2: ‘This Pūraṇa Kassapa leads an order and a community, and tutors a community. He’s a well-known and famous religious founder, deemed holy by many people.
translation_text.sn44.9:2.3: When a disciple passes away, he declares that
translation_text.sn44.9:2.4: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.
translation_text.sn44.9:2.5: And as for a disciple who is a supreme person, highest of people, having reached the highest point, when they pass away he also declares that
translation_text.sn44.9:2.6: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.
translation_text.sn44.9:3.2: the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan …
translation_text.sn44.9:3.5: Ajita of the hair blanket leads an order and a community, and tutors a community. He’s a well-known and famous religious founder, deemed holy by many people.
translation_text.sn44.9:3.6: When a disciple passes away, he declares that
translation_text.sn44.9:3.7: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.
translation_text.sn44.9:3.8: And as for a disciple who is a supreme person, highest of people, having reached the highest point, when they pass away he also declares that
translation_text.sn44.9:3.9: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.
translation_text.sn44.9:4.1: This ascetic Gotama leads an order and a community, and tutors a community. He’s a well-known and famous religious founder, deemed holy by many people.
translation_text.sn44.9:4.2: When a disciple passes away, he declares that
translation_text.sn44.9:4.3: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.
translation_text.sn44.9:4.4: But as for a disciple who is a supreme person, highest of people, having reached the highest point, when they pass away he doesn’t declare that
translation_text.sn44.9:4.5: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.
translation_text.sn44.9:4.6: Rather, he declares that they have
translation_text.sn44.9:4.7: cut off craving, cast off the fetters, and by rightly comprehending conceit have made an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn44.9:4.8: I had doubt and uncertainty about that:
translation_text.sn44.9:4.9: ‘How on earth can I understand the ascetic Gotama’s teaching?’”
translation_text.sn44.9:5.1: “Vaccha, no wonder you’re doubting and uncertain.
translation_text.sn44.9:5.2: Doubt has come up in you about an uncertain matter.
translation_text.sn44.9:5.3: I describe rebirth for someone who grasps fuel, not for someone who does not.
translation_text.sn44.9:5.4: It’s like a fire which only burns with fuel, not without fuel.
translation_text.sn44.9:5.5: In the same way I describe rebirth for someone who grasps fuel, not for someone who doesn’t grasp fuel.”
translation_text.sn44.9:6.1: “But when a flame is blown away by the wind, what does the worthy Gotama say is its fuel then?”
translation_text.sn44.9:6.2: “At such a time, I say that it’s fueled by the wind.
translation_text.sn44.9:6.3: For the wind is its fuel then.”
translation_text.sn44.9:6.4: “But when someone who is attached has laid down this body and has not been reborn in one of the realms, what does the worthy Gotama say is their fuel then?”
translation_text.sn44.9:6.5: “When someone who is attached has laid down this body, Vaccha, and has not been reborn in one of the realms, I say they’re fueled by craving.
translation_text.sn44.9:6.6: For craving is their fuel then.”

variant_text.sn44.9:3.3: sañcayo → sañjayo (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | belaṭṭhaputto → bellaṭṭhiputto (bj)
